# Agent Guide — PowerShell presence detector

The Windows/cross-platform twin of the [`bash`](../bash) detector: same presence-only contract,
implemented with built-in PowerShell cmdlets instead of `find` + `jq`.

## Run
```powershell
pwsh packages/powershell/repo-fingerprint.ps1 <path> [--format json|text] [--deep]
pwsh packages/powershell/repo-fingerprint.ps1 --list-ecosystems   # print matrix ecosystem ids
```
Env overrides: `RF_MATRIX`, `RF_SCHEMA` point at alternate `schema/` artifacts.
Requires **PowerShell 7+** (`pwsh`) — no external tools. Exit codes: `0` detected, `1` none,
`2` usage error.

## What it emits
A Detection Report with `generatedBy: "powershell"`. It is **presence-only** (byte-for-byte the same
detection as the bash twin): `rawScore`, `confidence` and `confidenceBucket` are `null`; `frameworks`
and `testing` are `[]`. Ecosystems, signals (`path`/`kind`/`weight`), `packageManagers`,
`buildTools`, `topology`, `infrastructure`, roles and `dominantEcosystem` are all populated.
`dominantEcosystem` = the ecosystem with the most root-level (`depth ≤ 1`) `primary-manifest`
signals (ties broken by id).

## Layout
- `repo-fingerprint.ps1` — CLI + scan + report assembly (built-in `ConvertFrom-Json`/`ConvertTo-Json`).
- `lib/matrix.ps1` — locates `schema/signal-matrix.json` / schema (dot-sourced by the CLI).
- `tests/exit-codes.ps1`, `tests/report.ps1` — self-contained tests (each exits non-zero on failure).

## Tests
```powershell
pwsh packages/powershell/tests/exit-codes.ps1
pwsh packages/powershell/tests/report.ps1      # validates output against the schema via the repo venv
```
Parity against the reference TypeScript detector is proven by `node scripts/parity.mjs` (the
PowerShell column is included automatically when `pwsh` is on PATH), with the same tolerances as the
bash impl (confidence / frameworks / testing excluded).
